title: cercocarpus paucidentatus
intro: Cercocarpus paucidentatus, also known as the Betulae Nut or Coyote Brush, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the family Rosaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cercocarpus paucidentatus include relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive issues and skin conditions. Cercocarpus paucidentatus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in herbal remedies.
